2606.06168.title: ProSarc: Prosody-Aware Sarcasm Recognition Framework via Temporal Prosodic Incongruity
2606.06168.paper_summary: We present ProSarc, an audio-only framework that detects sarcasm by modelling temporal prosodic incongruity, that is, the mismatch between local prosodic dynamics and the utterance-level emotional baseline. Dual encoding paths, a Global Emotion Encoder and a Temporal Prosody Encoder (BiLSTM + multi-head attention), feed a Prosodic Incongruity Analyzer that produces a scalar incongruity score for classification. Monte Carlo dropout provides uncertainty estimates, and an attention-based mechanism localises sarcastic onset without frame-level labels. ProSarc outperforms prior audio-only methods on MUStARD++ (F1=75.3) and generalises to spontaneous (PodSarc, F1=62.9) and cross-lingual speech (MuSaG, F1=65.6). Ten-run validation confirms the contribution of incongruity modelling (Wilcoxon p=0.002, Cohen's d=1.51). Human evaluation shows that model uncertainty tracks perceptual ambiguity and predicted onsets align with human-annotated temporal windows.
2606.06168.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了 ProSarc，一個僅使用音頻的框架，通過建模時間上的語調不一致性來檢測諷刺，即局部語調動態與發話級別情感基線之間的錯配。雙重編碼路徑，全球情感編碼器和時間語調編碼器（BiLSTM + 多頭注意力），為一個語調不一致性分析器提供數據，該分析器生成一個標量不一致性分數以進行分類。蒙特卡羅 dropout 提供不確定性估計，基於注意力的機制能夠在沒有幀級標籤的情況下定位諷刺的開始。ProSarc 在 MUStARD++ 上超越了先前的僅音頻方法（F1=75.3），並能夠推廣到自發性（PodSarc，F1=62.9）和跨語言語音（MuSaG，F1=65.6）。十次運行的驗證確認了不一致性建模的貢獻（Wilcoxon p=0.002，Cohen's d=1.51）。人類評估顯示，模型的不確定性與感知模糊性相符，預測的開始時間與人類標註的時間窗口一致。
2606.06168.author: Prathamjyot Singh et.al.
2606.06168.authors: Prathamjyot Singh, Ashima Sood, Sahil Sharma, Jasmeet Singh
